case.title: CANNATA v. CITY OF NEW YORK

opinion.h4: CANNATA v. CITY OF NEW YORK, (1962)
CANNATA ET AL. v. CITY OF NEW YORK. APPEAL FROM THE COURT OF APPEALS OF NEW YORK. No. 163. Decided October 8, 1962.
opinion.p: Appeal dismissed for want of a substantial federal question.
Leo A. Larkin and Pauline K. Berger for appellee.
The motion to dismiss is granted and the appeal is dismissed for want of a substantial federal question.
MR. JUSTICE GOLDBERG took no part in the consideration or decision of this case.
WAXMAN ET AL. v. VIRGINIA. APPEAL FROM THE SUPREME COURT OF APPEALS OF VIRGINIA. No. 274. Decided October 8, 1962.
Robert Y. Button, Attorney General of Virginia, and Kenneth C. Patty and D. Gardiner Tyler, Assistant Attorneys General, for appellee.
The judgments are reversed. San Diego Building Trades Council v. Garmon, 359 U.S. 236.
MR. JUSTICE GOLDBERG took no part in the consideration or decision of this case.
